name: Fizza
title: Loyal Servant of Ahl al-Bayt
shortDescription: The loyal maidservant of Lady Fatima (AS) who, after years of serving the Ahl al-Bayt, was present in Karbala and taken captive.
fullStory:
Fizza al-Nubiyya was the loyal maidservant and helper of Lady Fatima al-Zahra (AS). She was a very pious woman, a memorizer of the Quran, and a lover of the Ahl al-Bayt. Many stories of her miracles and eloquence have been narrated.

After the martyrdom of Lady Fatima (AS), Fizza remained in Amir al-Mu'minin's (AS) house and served the Prophet's (PBUH) children. Her loyalty was such that, despite her old age, she accompanied Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan to Karbala.

In the event of Karbala, Fizza witnessed the martyrdom of the dear ones she had helped raise since childhood. After Ashura, she too, along with Lady Zaynab (AS) and the other women, was taken captive by Ibn Sa'd's army.

It is narrated that on the eleventh night of Muharram, to protect the captives from Yazid's horsemen who intended to harass them, Fizza spoke to a fierce lion in the desert, and the lion took charge of protecting the half-burnt tent of the Ahl al-Bayt. She is the symbol of a lifetime of loyalty to the Prophet's (PBUH) family.
quoteArabic: ما كَلَّمَتْ فِضَّةُ فِي عِشْرينَ سَنَةً إِلّا بِالقُرْآنِ...
quote: (Describing her status:) For twenty whole years, Fizza spoke nothing but verses of the Quran...
